input.stack_trace:
java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "com.example.Order.getAmount()" because "order" is null
      at com.example.OrderService.total(OrderService.java:5)
      at com.example.OrderService.handle(OrderService.java:10)

conclusion.root_cause_hypothesis: OrderService.handle() passes a null Order reference into OrderService.total(), causing total() to dereference order when calling getAmount().
conclusion.counter_check: The exception message names 'order' as null at the dereference; the only caller frame shown is handle(), where order is explicitly set to null immediately before being passed, ruling out DAO/external data or getAmount() internals as the cause.
conclusion.fix_direction: Ensure handle() supplies a non-null Order to total(), or add null handling/validation in total() before dereferencing the parameter.
conclusion.confidence_reason: The complete null flow is visible in the provided source and stack frames: explicit null assignment, direct pass, and immediate dereference.
